bio: Afshon Ostovar is an associate professor at the Naval Postgraduate School in Monterey, California. He is the author of *Vanguard of the Imam: Religion, Politics, and Iran's Revolutionary Guards* (2016), widely regarded as the definitive English-language scholarly history of the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) — tracing its ideological origins, institutional development, and operational expansion from 1979 to the present. Ostovar situates the IRGC within the broader religious-ideological framework of the Islamic Republic, examining how its self-conception as the 'vanguard of the Imam' shaped both its internal culture and its external operations including the [Quds Force](/orgs/irgc-quds-force). His research spans Iranian security institutions, proxy networks, and the intersection of religion and military power.
editorial_notes: Author of Vanguard of the Imam (2016) — standard scholarly history of IRGC. Associate professor at NPS Monterey.
